title: Pouvoirs de signature de l'Afrique du Sud
text_preview: THEREAS, for the purpose, inter alia, of securing an increase in the number of National Committees on Intellectual Co-operation and their means of action, with the additional object of giving increased effect to the Agree- ment dated the 8th December, 1924, concluded between the Government of the French Republic and the League of Nations, and for the further purpose of increasing the present financial means availabl…

pages.text:
THEREAS, for the purpose, inter alia, of securing an increase in the number of

National Committees on Intellectual Co-operation and their means of action, with the additional object of giving increased effect to the Agree- ment dated the 8th December, 1924, concluded between the Government of the French Republic and the League of Nations, and for the further purpose of increasing the present financial means available for the work of the Inter- national Institute of Intellectual Co-operation, an International Act concerning Intellectual

Co-operation was concluded at Paris on December 3rd, 1938, and now lies open for signature by

Governments represented at the Conference convened for the purpose of concluding the said

Act;

AND WHEREAS it is expedient that a fit and proper person be

vested with full power to

sign the said Act on behalf of the Union

of

South Africa;

NOW THEREFORE, I, James Barry Munnik

Hertzog, Minister of External Affairs, of the

Union of South Africa, do hereby declare and make known that the Government of the Union of

2.

South Africa have authorised and appointed, and do by these Presents appoint ir. Sidney Frank

Materson, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister

Plenipotentiary of the Union of South Africa at

Paris, to be their Plenipotentiary with full parer and authority to sign the said Act on behalf of the Union of South Africa.

IN VITNESS WHEREOF I bave signed these

Presents at CAPE TOT on the twenty-first day of

March

Nine Hundred in the year of Our Lord, One Thousand and

Thirty

Nine.
